figures.caption: Fig. 3. Attention memory per classifier session vs. sequence length. SDPA exceeds the ∼718 MB available (dashed line) at ∼6K tokens. FA stays below but grows linearly. With compression, all inputs are reduced to 512 tokens, yielding a constant footprint regardless of original prompt length.
TABLE XIII GPU RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS OF LLM ROUTING SYSTEMS. OUR ROUTER IS THE ONLY ONE DESIGNED TO CO-LOCATE ON THE SERVING GPU.

TABLE I COMPARISON OF PROMPT COMPRESSION APPROACHES. COMPRESSION LATENCY IS FOR A 16K-TOKEN INPUT. OUR METHOD IS THE ONLY ONE REQUIRING ZERO NEURAL INFERENCE, KEEPING COMPRESSION OVERHEAD TO ∼19 MS AT 16K TOKENS VS. SECONDS FOR NEURAL METHODS.†

TABLE VIII STAGED ACCELERATION AT 8K TOKENS. EACH STAGE BUILDS ON THE PREVIOUS; PER-STAGE AND CUMULATIVE SPEEDUPS ARE MEASURED E2E THROUGH ENVOY (N=10 PER RUN, 2–3 RUNS; §V-A). THE 16K ROW SHOWS SCALING BEYOND THE CPU-OPERABLE REGIME.
Fig. 4. E2E latency comparison (log scale). GPU+FA with streaming and compression achieves 50 ms at 8K tokens. At 16K+, only GPU+FA operates (108 ms at 16K; Table VIII).

TABLE X CLASSIFIER LATENCY UNDER CONCURRENT LOAD ON MI300X: RAW FA (TOP) VS. EFFECTIVE WITH COMPRESSION + STREAMING (BOTTOM). WITH COMPRESSION ALL INPUTS REDUCE TO ∼512 TOKENS. BOTTOM-PANEL C=1 VALUES AT 512–16K ARE MEASURED; 32K C=1 IS ESTIMATED. C>1 VALUES ARE DERIVED FROM C=1 OVERHEAD PLUS THE 512-TOKEN FA CONCURRENCY PROFILE.
